data.title: Bitcoin miners offload 15K BTC since October, with more sales expected
data.description:
Public miners are trimming Bitcoin reserves as tightening margins, debt pressure and a post-crash reset force the industry to rethink its once-popular hold strategy.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-miners-unwind-btc-treasuries-margin-pressure?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Ukraine's Oil Pipeline May Resume Operations in Six Weeks, Says Zelensky
data.description:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced that the 'Friendship' oil pipeline, damaged in a Russian attack in January, could be operational again in about six weeks. According to RTHK, the pipeline previously transported Russian oil through Ukraine to Hungary and Slovakia. However, Ukraine reported that the pipeline was damaged during a Russian assault, halting oil transport since late January. Hungary and Slovakia have rejected Ukraine's claims, accusing Ukraine of deliberately delaying the pipeline's restoration. Hungary has also taken steps to block European Union aid to Ukraine.

During a press conference in Kyiv on Thursday, Zelensky expressed reluctance to restart the pipeline, as it carries Russian oil. However, he acknowledged that if reopening the pipeline is the only way to unfreeze critical EU aid plans, Kyiv might have no other choice. Zelensky stated that repair work on the pipeline could be completed within a month and a half, and Ukraine would be fully prepared for its resumption.

data.title: U.S. Money Market Fund Assets Reach Record $7.82 Trillion
data.description: The Investment Company Institute (ICI) reports that U.S. money market fund assets have surged to an unprecedented $7.82 trillion. According to Jin10, this milestone reflects growing investor interest in money market funds amid economic uncertainties. The increase in assets highlights a shift in investor behavior, as many seek safer investment options in the current financial climate. This trend underscores the broader market sentiment, where caution prevails among investors navigating volatile economic conditions. The record-high asset level indicates a significant capital flow into money market funds, suggesting a preference for stability and liquidity.

data.title: New Zealand Reopens Luxury Property Market to Foreign Investors Amid Geopolitical Tensions
data.description: New Zealand is welcoming wealthy foreign investors back into its luxury property market, responding to increased demand driven by geopolitical tensions. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the country's strategic move to attract international buyers seeking secure investments in remote locations. This decision comes as global uncertainties prompt affluent individuals to explore safe havens for their assets. The reopening of the market is expected to boost New Zealand's real estate sector, offering opportunities for high-net-worth individuals to invest in exclusive properties. The initiative aligns with the nation's broader economic strategy to leverage its appeal as a stable and attractive destination for foreign capital.

data.title: Revoult, ZeroHash Apply for U.S. Banking Charters
data.description:
The two crypto-adjacent firms want to deepen their ties to regulated U. S. financial systems with banking licenses.
source: https://www.bankless.com/read/news/revoult-zerohash-apply-for-u-s-banking-charters

data.title: U.S. Aircraft Crashes in Iraq's Basra Province
data.description: A U.S. aircraft has reportedly crashed in Iraq's Basra Province, according to Kurdish media outlet Rudaw English. According to Jin10, Basra police have confirmed the incident and stated that officials are currently searching for the pilot. Further details about the cause of the crash or the condition of the pilot have not been disclosed at this time. The situation is developing, and more information is expected to be released as the investigation continues.

data.title: Bitcoin Holdings on Exchanges Reach Lowest Level Since 2018
data.description: Bitcoin holdings on centralized exchanges have decreased to less than 2,708,000 BTC as of March 5. According to NS3.AI, this represents the lowest level of exchange Bitcoin balances since November 2018, based on data from Unfolded.

data.title: Royal Bank of Canada CEO's Compensation Surges with Record Earnings
data.description: Royal Bank of Canada CEO Dave McKay received a total compensation of C$23.76 million last year. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this pay package included a bonus that was 86% above his target, reflecting the firm's achievement of record earnings. The significant increase in McKay's compensation underscores the bank's strong financial performance over the period.

data.title: Penguin Apparel Brand Targets Crypto IP Pudgy Penguins in Trademark Infringement Lawsuit
data.description:
The firm behind the Original Penguin brand is suing Pudgy Penguins in a lawsuit that alleges the crypto brand infringes on its trademarks.
source: https://decrypt.co/360169/penguin-apparel-brand-targets-pudgy-trademark-infringement-lawsuit

data.title: House Committee Advances Bills to Enhance Online Safety for Children
data.description: A key House committee has moved forward with a series of bills designed to enhance the safety of children on the internet. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the committee's significant effort this year to regulate popular platforms like Instagram and TikTok. The legislative package seeks to address concerns about the impact of social media on young users, aiming to implement stricter safety measures and guidelines. Lawmakers are increasingly focused on protecting children from harmful content and ensuring their privacy online. The proposed bills reflect a growing consensus on the need for more robust regulations to safeguard minors in the digital age. As the bills progress, they will undergo further scrutiny and debate, with the goal of establishing comprehensive protections for children using social media platforms.

data.title: Venezuela Expected to Exceed Oil Production Targets, Says U.S. Interior Secretary Burgum
data.description: U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum expressed confidence that Venezuela will surpass its oil production goals. According to Jin10, Burgum highlighted the potential for increased output from the South American nation, which could have significant implications for global oil markets. Venezuela's efforts to boost production come amid ongoing challenges in the energy sector, including geopolitical tensions and fluctuating demand. The country's ability to meet and exceed these targets could influence oil prices and impact international trade dynamics.

data.description:

source: https://coincu.com/news/revolut-seeks-u-s-bank-charter-amid-bid-for-fed-access/

The deal, priced at the Secured Overnight Financing Rate plus […]

data.title: Amazon Experiences Technical Issues in the U.S.
data.description: Amazon users in the United States have reported technical issues, according to DownDetector. The website, which monitors network conditions, indicated that several users experienced disruptions while accessing Amazon's services. The nature and extent of the issues remain unclear, and Amazon has yet to release an official statement addressing the situation. Users are advised to stay updated through official channels for further information.

data.title: Is Ethereum Waking Up? Binance ETH Turnover Hits 6-Month High as Volatility Returns
data.description:
Analysts say high exchange turnover often reflects traders repositioning portfolios quickly during periods of rising volatility.
source: https://cryptopotato.com/is-ethereum-waking-up-binance-eth-turnover-hits-6-month-high-as-volatility-returns/

data.title: Wyoming Is Putting Public Money Into a Bitcoin Treasury Company
data.description:
Wyoming’s State Treasurer disclosed a purchase of 250,000 shares of Strive Inc. on March 5, 2026, marking the first time a U. S. state has directly invested public funds into a publicly traded company that holds Bitcoin as its primary treasury reserve asset
source: https://www.ethnews.com/wyoming-is-putting-public-money-into-a-bitcoin-treasury-company/

data.title: U.S. Treasury to Announce Measures to Address Rising Energy Prices
data.description: The U.S. Treasury is expected to announce measures as early as Thursday to address the surge in energy prices, according to a senior White House official. According to Jin10, these measures may include utilizing the oil futures market to mitigate the impact of rising costs. The announcement comes amid growing concerns over the economic implications of escalating energy prices, which have been affecting various sectors. The Treasury's actions are anticipated to provide some relief and stability to the market, although specific details of the measures have not yet been disclosed. The move reflects the administration's ongoing efforts to manage economic challenges and support the energy sector.

data.title: Baltex Speeds Monero‑TRON Swaps to 20 Minutes
data.description:
Baltex. io now allows Monero-to-TRON swaps in around 20 minutes, combining privacy, speed, and non-custodial control across multiple blockchain networks.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/baltex-speeds-monero-tron-swaps-to-20-minutes/
